content.text:
Sertraline was first approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in **1991**. It was developed by Pfizer and marketed under the brand name **Zoloft**.

It became one of the most widely prescribed antidepressants, belonging to the class of medications known as selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs). Following its U.S. approval, it was subsequently approved and marketed in many other countries throughout the 1990s.
